name: Österreichische Nationalbibliothek (Austrian National Library)
description: The beautiful baroque library of the Hapsburg empire.

name: Stephansdom Crypt
description: The crypt of the imposing Stephensdom holds royal intestines and thousands of skeletons.

name: Habsburg Imperial Crypt
description: The final resting place for the remains of 143 Habsburg royalty.

name: Schmetterlinghaus: The Imperial Butterfly Park
description: A tropical paradise just a short trip from the opera house is filled with beautiful winged creatures.

name: Clock Museum
description: A small museum filled with over 1,000 clocks, and one particular clock calibrated to run until the year 9999.

name: Karlskirche (St. Charles's Church)
description: Built to give thanks for the passing of a plague epidemic, this cathedral is a one-of-a-kind architectural hybrid.

name: Kriminalmuseum
description: Macabre museum devoted to historical Viennese murders.

name: The Narrenturm
description: This former insane asylum now holds a pathological anatomy museum.

name: Palmenhaus
description: Dine amidst exotic plants at this former royal greenhouse.

name: The Lost Garden of Schloss Schonbrunn
description: The topiary maze at the Habsburg hunting lodge.

name: Sigmund Freud Museum
description: The former private quarters and office of Dr. Sigmund Freud now hosts a museum housing the works of the founding father of psychoanalysis.

name: Republic of Kugelmugel
description: A spherical "micro-nation" in the heart of Vienna.

name: Flohmarkt Lager
description: Inside the corridors of this flea market, visitors are sure to find some obscure and odd treasures.

name: Hundertwasser's Odd Architecture
description: Op-art architecture designed for happiness.

name: Museum of Art Fakes
description: This Austrian museum celebrates the work of famous forgers and their inimitable imitations.

name: Globe Museum
description: Handsomely crafted representations of our round planet.

name: Wotruba Church
description: This cubist Vienna church building looks like some sort of alien stonehenge.

name: Jubiläumswarte
description: Located right outside of Vienna, visitors can take in expansive views of an ethereal forest.

name: Vienna Sewer System
description: Vienna's underworld of tunnels and subterranean rivers made an iconic appearance in a 1949 Orson Welles film.

name: Schloss Hellbrunn
description: Trick water gardens from 1619 featuring a 200 piece mechanical theater powered by water.

name: Cemetery of the Nameless
description: The resting place for anonymous victims of the Danube's dark waters.

name: Naturhistorisches Museum
description: Beautiful Austrian natural history museum with carved ceilings and row after row of ancient taxidermy.

name: Wiener Pestsäule (Vienna Plague Column)
description: Baroque celebration of the end of the Great Plague of Vienna.

name: Crystal Worlds
description: Swarovski's museum/theme park is a fake diamond fantasia overlooked by a giant.

name: Wolf and Cow Playing Backgammon Mural
description: A silly medieval mural preserved on the side of a Viennese house.

name: Museum of Contraception and Abortion
description: This museum promotes informed contraception by documenting its often secret past.

name: Justizcafe
description: A rooftop restaurant hides in plain sight atop Vienna's Palace of Justice.

name: Votivkirche (Votive Church)
description: A neo-Gothic church built to thank God for saving the Emperor of Austria from an assassination.

name: 'Stock-im-Eisen' Nail Tree
description: Legends surround an ancient tree trunk riddled with nails for good luck.

name: Josephinum Medical Museum
description: One of the best collections of wax anatomical models in the world.

name: Ungarisches Haus (Hungarian House)
description: The townhouse where the "Blood Countess" Elizabeth Báthory started her murderous career.

name: Vienna Grand Ferris Wheel
description: Vienna's giant wonder wheel had a starring role in one of the greatest noir movies of all time.

name: Vienna Grand Ferris Wheel
description: Vienna's giant wonder wheel had a starring role in one of the greatest noir movies of all time.

name: Supersense
description: A neo-Gothic mansion now houses a wunderkammer of analog tech that hits on all the senses.

name: Kunstkammer Wien
description: The Habsburgs' royal collections of art and wonders.

name: St. Sebastian’s Cemetery
description: Terrifically morbid cemetary and site of famous Paracelsus' grave.

name: Bestattungsmuseum (Vienna Undertakers' Museum)
description: Collection dedicated to funeral rites, equipment, and mourning rituals of the Viennese.

name: Vollpension
description: Visiting this café is like visiting the Austrian grandma you wish you had.

name: Flak Towers
description: Giant WWII fortresses that fired 8,000 rounds a minute, now home to thousands of pigeons.

name: Ampelpärchen
description: Vienna's diversity-themed traffic lights were inspired by the Eurovision Song Contest.

name: Eisriesenwelt
description: The largest ice cave in the world is thought to be the entrance to hell according to Austrian legend.

name: Riegersburg Castle
description: A centuries old castle atop a dormant volcano houses museums devoted to both women and witches.

name: Jesuitenkirche (Jesuit Church)
description: The solemn exterior conceals an opulent interior of this 17th century church.

name: Hallstatt Charnel House
description: An Austrian house of bones filled with hundreds of intricately painted skulls.

name: Eggenburg Charnel
description: Artfully arranged bones of 5,800 Austrians.

name: Green Lake
description: Each year melting snow turns these Austrian hiking trails into scuba zones.

name: Dominikanerkirche (Dominican Church)
description: An exquisite Baroque church in Vienna's old town.

name: Museumsquartier Passages
description: This series of Baroque passageways in Vienna's cultural center mix old architecture with modern art.

name: Esperanto Museum
description: Museum devoted to the artificial language of Esperanto.

name: Otto Wagner Villa I (Ernst Fuchs Museum)
description: This grand manse has been transformed into a museum devoted to one of the founders of Fantastic Realism.

name: The Stairs of Reconciliation
description: Double spiral staircases are not unheard of, but they are very rare.

name: Grottenbahn
description: Ride a dragon train into a world of dwarves.

name: Zwerglgarten (Dwarf Garden)
description: Ugly 18th-century dwarf statues living in a perfectly landscaped Austrian garden.

name: Aqua Dome Langenfeld
description: Visitors dip into a variety of pools of what was once ice.

name: Windows For Peace
description: On a winding Vienna side street, one museum has turned their windows into a monument to history’s peace-minded heroes.

name: Seegrotte
description: This series of underground chambers contains Europe's largest subterranean lake that is slowly drained each day.

name: Wehrmann in Eisen Nail Man
description: A patriotic medieval knight in driven-nail armor raised money for widows and orphans of WWI.

name: Fucking, Austria
description: American soldiers discovered Europe's best one-liner at the end of World War II.

name: Hofjagd und Rüstkammer
description: A magnificent collection of imperial armor and weaponry.

name: Karl Marx-Hof
description: The housing complex served as an anti-fascist fortress during the Austrian Civil War.

name: The Catacombs of St. Peter's Abbey
description: These 12th-century catacombs carved into a mountainside were a place of mysticism and Christian hermitages.

name: Nonseum
description: The world's largest collection of intentionally useless inventions.

name: Römermuseum
description: This museum is dedicated to the little known ancient Roman history of Vienna.

name: Neidhart Frescoes
description: A glimpse into the festive and private lives of medieval Europe.

name: Salzwelten: The Hallstatt Salt Mine
description: The world's oldest known salt mine.

name: Murinsel
description: Resting in the middle of an Austrian river, this sci-fi "island" lets people cross in neon style.

name: Hofpavillon Hietzing
description: An ornate private entrance to the Vienna subway built specifically for the emperor.

name: Burgruine Gösting (Gösting Castle Ruins)
description: The shell of a medieval fortress offers commanding views of the area it once protected.

name: Mozart's Skull
description: Mozart's skull may be held in this collection, but definitive proof continues to elude scientists.

name: Brennpunkt°
description: A history of heat is on display at this unique Austrian museum.

name: Chamber of Art and Curiosities
description: Archduke Ferdinand II's collection of rare objects is considered one of the oldest museums in the world.

name: 'Gurken'
description: Amidst medieval and baroque buildings, five human-sized gherkins stand tall.

name: Almkanal of Salzburg
description: The medieval network of canals hidden underneath the city is opened to explorers each year.

name: St. Florian Ossuary
description: The personal charnel house of Anton Bruckner.

name: Hochstrahlbrunnen
description: Along the edge of this fountain are 365 smaller fountains that represent the days of the year.

name: Café Sperl
description: Royals, rebels, and artists have all conversed at this grand 19th-century Viennese coffee house.

name: Gsellmann's Weltmaschine
description: "World machine" built by an untrained Austrian farmer.

name: Dom Museum's Kunst und Wunderkammer
description: The amazing wonder cabinets of a very rich Austrian archbishop.

name: Rathaus Paternoster
description: A non-stop elevator ride in Vienna's city hall.

name: Panorama Museum Salzburg
description: This 19th century cyclorama acts like an analogue time machine to a different era.

name: Ausgrabungen Michaelerplatz
description: In the middle of a Vienna square, Roman ruins dating back some 2,000 years are on display.

name: 5 Fingers Observation Deck
description: A breathtaking viewing platform over the Alps.

name: Meteorological Pillar of the 19th Century
description: The beautiful, ornate weather station is the most popular geocache in Salzburg.

name: The Altar of the Basilica of St. Michael
description: This skeleton-filled altar piece served as a backdrop to one of the most iconic scenes in "The Sound of Music."

name: Steinhof Church
description: The first modernist church in Europe.

name: Kunsthaus Graz
description: Austria's ultra-modern art museum looks like Cthulhu's giant alien heart.

name: The World of Puppets Museum
description: A small marionette collection located within the dark cellars of an 11th-century Austrian castle.

name: Wien Museum Hermesvilla
description: A gift from Emperor Franz Joseph I to his wife.

name: Boltzmann's Grave
description: Physicist’s epitaph provides final confirmation to a career of turmoil.

name: Fairytale Grotto Line
description: Fantastic stories on a miniature railroad.

name: Zwentendorf Nuclear Power Plant
description: Austria's very first and only nuclear power station was completed but never used.

name: Dasparkhotel
description: A unique opportunity to stay the night in retrofitted sections of sewer pipe.

name: Gugging House of Artists
description: This outsider art house has gone from clinical creation to astounding artwork.

name: 'Seen des Himmels' ('Lakes of the Sky')
description: In the fictional world of Kcymaerxthaere, standing at this site just outside Vienna, Austria, the sky would have been found beneath your feet.

name: Welt der Gifte
description: At this exhibit on the world of poison, venomous animals steal the show.

name: Pyramidenkogel Observation Tower
description: This spire on an Austrian mountain is wrapped around a long spiral slide and has the world's tallest wooden observation deck.

name: Kristallhütte
description: Part ski resort, part modern art space, this lodge brings giant metal spiders and bloody doors to the Austrian Alps.

name: Gugumuck Wiener Schneckenmanufaktur
description: An IT-specialist-turned-farmer reintroduces an Austrian food tradition while promoting snails as a sustainable "future food" option.

name: Tyrolean Folk Art Museum
description: Devilish masks, handicrafts, and pieces of old buildings create a snapshot of life in a historic Alpine region.

name: Minimundus
description: The world's most important buildings shrunk to a tiny size.

name: St. Peter Stiftskulinarium
description: Epicurean and holy traditions converge in what may be the oldest restaurant in Europe.

name: WIENXTRA-Spielebox
description: Journey through a mesmerizing array of board games inside this museum.

name: Castle Itter
description: German and American troops joined forces to defend this fortress during the strangest battle of World War II.

name: The Cemetery Library
description: A three-shelf library serves as a memorial in the Jewish cemetery of Krems.

name: Styrian Armoury
description: The world's largest historic armory was established by independent citizens to defend themselves from the wars of larger forces.

name: Memorial Against War and Fascism
description: Designed as a reminder of one of the darkest moments in Austrian history.

name: Strudlhofstiege
description: A set of outdoor stairs named in honor of a famous Austrian artist.

name: The Empty Tomb of Maximilian I
description: This splendid tomb may look fantastic, but there's nothing in it...

name: Vienna Beethoven Museum
description: A museum capturing one of the world's most famous classical composer's battle with hearing loss.

name: Brauerei Hofstetten
description: Austria’s oldest brewery makes beer with flaming-hot granite.

name: Hommage to Mozart
description: This strange statue features a bust of the famous composer plopped atop a woman's body.

name: Austrian State Archives
description: More than a millennium's worth of municipal records preserve the political history of Austria.

name: Geteilte Kirche am Kreuzbichl (Divided Church)
description: A road runs through the middle of this small Austrian chapel.

name: Church of St. Barbara
description: Bizarrely-painted church designed by a controversial artist.

name: Blutgassenviertel
description: One of Vienna’s oldest (and perhaps most haunted) alleys, allegedly with a bloody past that gave it its name.

name: Mauthausen Memorial
description: The site of Austria's biggest concentration camp now honors its victims.

name: Klo & So Museum of Historic Sanitary Objects
description: These are probably the most beautifully crafted toilets you've ever seen.

name: Lindwurmbrunnen
description: The medieval "dragon" on an Austrian city's coat-of-arms comes to life in a fountain.

name: Stubentor Metro Station City Wall Remains
description: These relics of the city's past now exist as a prominent modern feature.

name: Stargate Kappern
description: Is this a portal to another side of the cosmos?

name: Waldviertel Pyramid
description: A pyramid in Central Europe of unknown origin.

name: Computer und Gaming Museum Wien
description: Housed in a former air raid shelter, this museum is dedicated to the history of computing and video games.

name: Mayerling Lodge
description: Site of the 1889 murder-suicide of crown prince Rudolf and his lover Mary Vetsera.

name: Altaussee Salt Mines
description: During World War II, Austria's oldest salt mine was used to store thousands of pieces of stolen artwork.

name: Vienna Cloth Measures
description: These two 15th-century metal rods allowed people to check if they were swindled or not.

name: Vienna Arsenal (Military History Museum)
description: The world’s oldest military museum has remained largely unchanged since the last days of the Austro-Hungarian Empire.

name: Kremsmunster Observatory
description: This "mathematical tower" was an observatory, early skyscraper, and place to reflect on all of nature and the cosmos.

name: Mariazellerbahn
description: Narrow-gauge railway with its own personal power grid.

name: Pfarrkirche Malta (Maria Hilf Assumptio)
description: This medieval church in a small Austrian village is home to a 700-year-old Mickey Mouse lookalike.

name: Herzgruft 'Heart Crypt'
description: The hearts of 54 Habsburg royals received special burials here separate from their bodies.

name: Uhrenstube Aschau
description: One man's little-known private clock museum hides inside a historic farmhouse, set amidst the Austrian countryside.

name: Justizzentrum Leoben
description: Austria's 5-star prison.

name: Çerkez Dayı (Uncle Circassian)
description: A monument dedicated to a lone, brave invader.

name: Festenburg Fortress
description: Home of controversial poet priest who penned the words for the Austrian national anthem used in the 1930s.

name: Statue of Basilio Calafati
description: One of the most notable figures in the Wurstelprater’s history is often assumed to be a fictional character.

name: Gedenkstätte der Anatomie
description: A memorial to those who donated their bodies to science.

name: Handwerkerdörfl Pichla
description: This open-air museum filled with 19th-century buildings offers a unique view of historic craftsmanship.

name: Virgilkapelle (St Virgil’s Chapel)
description: Rediscovered underground chapel is one of the best examples of Gothic architecture in Vienna.

name: Hallstatt Museum
description: Dedicated to this region's early settlements and rich history of salt mining.

name: Römerstadt Carnuntum (Roman City Carnuntum)
description: A rebuilt Roman city that was once the largest Roman settlement in the region.

name: Eisenberg Lawn Cross
description: Cross mysteriously appeared for 36 years.

name: Luftg'selchter Pfarrer (The Air-Dried Priest)
description: In a crypt underneath this church lies a naturally mummified vicar whose story is surrounded by mystery.

name: Der Kleine Elefant
description: This small, easily-overlooked sculpture recalls the first time Graz saw a live elephant.

name: Bambi House
description: Vienna’s most overlooked celebrity is a Disney star.

name: Enns Stadtturm (Enns Tower)
description: A secret hotel room can be found inside this clocktower in the oldest city in Austria.

name: Rauchfangkehrermuseum (Wieden District Museum)
description: Celebrate the history of the lucky chimney sweep.

name: Mozarts Pinkelstein (Mozart's Pee Stone)
description: A monument marks the site where the famed composer stopped to relieve himself on a long journey. Just one problem: Mozart never went to Raschala.

name: 007 Elements
description: A mountaintop museum of Bond props, sets, and cinematic secrets.

name: Vienna’s Public Scales
description: Sturdy scales are a surprising thing to find in a modern European city.

name: Nähmaschinenmuseum Madersperger
description: The father of the sewing machine died broke, but this small, free museum is dedicated to the unsung inventor.

name: Sennhütte
description: Cozy mountain lodge home to a world record-winning collection of edelweiss flowers.

name: House Attack
description: The house that took a plunge into the facade of a museum.

name: Starkenberger Beer Pools
description: Where beer lovers can literally immerse themselves in beer.

name: Vienna Love Parade
description: Annual technopop celebration of peace and love.
